user_message: I have this escalation email from Tailspin Toys that needs run the hygiene check. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 14:28 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 11:32 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 14:31 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-K trap (Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-K failure mode (Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-K -- Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
